{
  "name": "studioforge",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "private": true,
  "workspaces": ["apps/*", "packages/*"],
  "scripts": {
    "build": "npm run build --workspaces --if-present",
    "dev": "docker compose up -d postgres redis minio && npm run dev --workspaces --if-present",
    "lint": "eslint . --ext .ts,.tsx",
    "typecheck": "npm run typecheck --workspaces --if-present",
    "test": "npm run test --workspaces --if-present",
    "migrate": "psql \"$DATABASE_URL\" -v ON_ERROR_STOP=1 -f migrations/001_initial.sql",
    "seed": "psql \"$DATABASE_URL\" -v ON_ERROR_STOP=1 -f packages/db/seed.sql",
    "format": "prettier --write .",
    "prepare": "husky"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@eslint/js": "9.17.0",
    "@types/node": "22.10.2",
    "eslint": "9.17.0",
    "eslint-config-prettier": "9.1.0",
    "husky": "9.1.7",
    "prettier": "3.4.2",
    "tsx": "4.19.2",
    "typescript": "5.7.2",
    "typescript-eslint": "8.18.1",
    "vitest": "2.1.8"
  },
  "engines": { "node": ">=22.0.0", "npm": ">=10.0.0" }
}

{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "target": "ES2022",
    "module": "NodeNext",
    "moduleResolution": "NodeNext",
    "strict": true,
    "noUncheckedIndexedAccess": true,
    "exactOptionalPropertyTypes": true,
    "esModuleInterop": true,
    "skipLibCheck": true,
    "declaration": true,
    "sourceMap": true,
    "resolveJsonModule": true
  }
}
{ "singleQuote": true, "trailingComma": "all", "printWidth": 100 }

node_modules
dist
coverage
.env

cypress/screenshots
cypress/videos
*.tfstate*
.terraform

NODE_ENV=development
DATABASE_URL=postgresql://studioforge:studioforge@localhost:5432/studioforge
REDIS_URL=redis://localhost:6379
JWT_SECRET=replace-with-at-least-32-random-characters
ACCESS_TOKEN_TTL=15m
REFRESH_TOKEN_DAYS=30
AWS_REGION=us-east-1
S3_BUCKET=studioforge-dev
S3_ENDPOINT=http://localhost:9000
AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID=minio
AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=miniosecret
AI_GATEWAY_URL=http://localhost:4001
AI_PROVIDER_URL=
AI_PROVIDER_API_KEY=
SENTRY_DSN=
WEB_ORIGIN=http://localhost:5173

services:
  postgres:
    image: postgres:16.6-alpine
    environment:
      POSTGRES_USER: studioforge
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: studioforge
      POSTGRES_DB: studioforge
    ports: ["5432:5432"]
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U studioforge"]
      interval: 5s
      timeout: 3s
      retries: 10
  redis:
    image: redis:7.4.1-alpine
    command: redis-server --appendonly yes
    ports: ["6379:6379"]
  minio:
    image: minio/minio:RELEASE.2024-12-18T13-15-44Z
    command: server /data --console-address :9001
    environment:
      MINIO_ROOT_USER: minio
      MINIO_ROOT_PASSWORD: miniosecret
    ports: ["9000:9000", "9001:9001"]
{ "name": "@studioforge/shared-types", "version": "1.0.0", "type": "module", "exports": "./src/index.ts" }
{ "extends": "../../tsconfig.base.json", "compilerOptions": { "outDir": "dist", "rootDir": "src" }, "include": ["src/**/*.ts"] }

{ "name": "@studioforge/ai-gateway", "version": "1.0.0", "type": "module", "scripts": { "dev": "tsx watch src/server.ts", "build": "tsc", "start": "node dist/server.js", "typecheck": "tsc --noEmit" }, "dependencies": { "fastify": "5.2.0", "ioredis": "5.4.1", "pg": "8.13.1" }, "devDependencies": { "@types/pg": "8.11.10" } }
{ "extends": "../../tsconfig.base.json", "compilerOptions": { "outDir": "dist", "rootDir": "src" }, "include": ["src/**/*.ts"] }

{ "name": "@studioforge/workers", "version": "1.0.0", "type": "module", "scripts": { "dev": "tsx watch src/worker.ts", "build": "tsc", "start": "node dist/worker.js", "typecheck": "tsc --noEmit" }, "dependencies": { "bullmq": "5.34.3", "ioredis": "5.4.1", "pg": "8.13.1" }, "devDependencies": { "@types/pg": "8.11.10" } }
{ "extends": "../../tsconfig.base.json", "compilerOptions": { "outDir": "dist", "rootDir": "src" }, "include": ["src/**/*.ts"] }

{ "name": "@studioforge/web-frontend", "version": "1.0.0", "private": true, "type": "module", "scripts": { "dev": "vite --host 0.0.0.0", "build": "tsc -b && vite build", "typecheck": "tsc --noEmit" }, "dependencies": { "@tanstack/react-query": "5.62.7", "react": "19.0.0", "react-dom": "19.0.0" }, "devDependencies": { "@types/react": "19.0.3", "@types/react-dom": "19.0.2", "@vitejs/plugin-react": "4.3.4", "vite": "6.0.5" } }
{ "extends": "../../tsconfig.base.json", "compilerOptions": { "module": "ESNext", "moduleResolution": "Bundler", "jsx": "react-jsx", "noEmit": true }, "include": ["src"] }

:root{font-family:Inter,system-ui,sans-serif;color:#111827;background:#f3f4f6}*{box-sizing:border-box}body{margin:0}main{max-width:960px;margin:auto;padding:clamp(1rem,4vw,3rem)}header{background:#111827;color:white;padding:2rem;border-radius:1rem}section{background:white;margin-top:1rem;padding:1.5rem;border-radius:.75rem;box-shadow:0 1px 3px #0002}label{display:block;font-weight:650;margin:.75rem 0}input{display:block;width:100%;max-width:32rem;padding:.75rem;margin-top:.3rem;border:2px solid #6b7280;border-radius:.4rem;font:inherit}input:focus,button:focus{outline:3px solid #60a5fa;outline-offset:2px}button{background:#1d4ed8;color:white;border:0;border-radius:.4rem;padding:.8rem 1.2rem;font-weight:700}button:disabled{background:#6b7280}@media(min-width:700px){section{padding:2rem}}
{ "name": "@studioforge/ts-sdk", "version": "1.0.0", "type": "module", "exports": "./src/index.ts" }

export class StudioForgeClient { constructor(private readonly baseUrl:string,private readonly token:()=>string){} private async request<T>(path:string,init?:RequestInit):Promise<T>{const response=await fetch(`${this.baseUrl.replace(/\/$/,'')}${path}`,{...init,headers:{'content-type':'application/json',authorization:`Bearer ${this.token()}`,...init?.headers}});const body=await response.json();if(!response.ok)throw Object.assign(new Error(body.message),body);return body as T;} createProject(orgId:string,input:{title:string;timezone:string;start_date?:string;end_date?:string}){return this.request(`/orgs/${orgId}/projects`,{method:'POST',body:JSON.stringify(input)});} getJob(jobId:string){return this.request<{id:string;status:string;result?:unknown}>(`/jobs/${jobId}`);} }

# Canonical Role and Permission Matrix

| Capability | Admin | Producer | Finance | Crew | Member | Viewer |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| Organization settings and user administration | Manage | Read | Read | No | No | No |
| Projects, scripts, scenes, locations | Manage | Manage | Read | Edit assigned | Edit | Read |
| People and availability | Manage | Manage | Read | Edit self | Edit | Read |
| Schedules and locks | Manage | Manage | Read | Edit assigned | Comment | Read |
| Budgets and cost items | Manage | Read | Manage | No | No | No |
| Assets and versions | Manage | Manage | Read | Upload | Upload | Read |
| Comments and notifications | Manage | Manage | Comment | Comment | Comment | Read |
| Approval requests | Manage | Manage | Manage finance | Read | Read | Read |
| Approval decisions | Manage | Manage | Manage finance | No | No | No |
| Audit logs | Read | No | No | No | No | No |
| AI opt-in and quotas | Manage | Use when enabled | Use for estimates | No | Use when enabled | No |

All permissions are constrained to the authenticated organization. Admin and Finance users require TOTP MFA. API authorization and PostgreSQL RLS are both mandatory; UI visibility is not an authorization control.

# Entity relationship diagram

```mermaid
erDiagram
  organizations ||--o{ users : contains
  organizations ||--o{ projects : owns
  users ||--o{ sessions : authenticates
  projects ||--o{ scripts : contains
  scripts ||--o{ script_parses : versions
  scripts ||--o{ scenes : extracts
  projects ||--o{ locations : uses
  projects ||--o{ schedules : plans
  schedules ||--o{ shoot_days : contains
  shoot_days ||--o{ schedule_assignments : assigns
  people ||--o{ availability_windows : declares
  people ||--o{ schedule_assignments : works
  projects ||--o{ budgets : funds
  budgets ||--o{ budget_items : contains
  projects ||--o{ assets : stores
  assets ||--o{ asset_versions : versions
  users ||--o{ comments : authors
  users ||--o{ notifications : receives
  organizations ||--o{ approvals : governs
  organizations ||--o{ audit_logs : audits
  organizations ||--o{ ai_requests : accounts
  organizations ||--o{ jobs : queues
```

Concrete types, foreign keys, checks, indexes, RLS policies, and the dashboard materialized view are canonical in `migrations/001_initial.sql`.

# AI governance and prompt templates

AI is disabled unless `organizations.settings.ai_enabled` is true. The gateway redacts email addresses, phone numbers, and government identifiers before provider submission. External submission requires recorded organizational consent. Requests are limited by monthly character budget and per-request size, cached by redacted input hash, template ID, model version, and requested output length.

## script-summary-v1

System instruction: summarize only facts present in the screenplay. Short output is two or three sentences; long output is one paragraph. Never infer protected traits, legal conclusions, or production facts absent from input.

Every response records model, template, redacted prompt hash, timestamp, token usage, cache status, response hash, and cost accounting. Provider outages return a stable 503 error and do not bypass the gateway.

# Operations runbooks

## Incident response

Acknowledge the alert, preserve correlation IDs and traces, declare severity, disable affected ingress or tenant, rotate exposed credentials through Secrets Manager and KMS, notify security and affected customers, preserve immutable audit evidence, remediate, and publish a reviewed post-incident report.

## Tenant isolation

Disable the organization at ingress, revoke all sessions in PostgreSQL and Redis, inspect RLS claims and audit hashes, block storage prefixes, preserve evidence, test another organization for leakage, and restore access only after security approval.

## Backup and restore

Shared PostgreSQL receives daily encrypted logical backups; dedicated databases use provider snapshots. Buckets have versioning and lifecycle protection. Backups are tagged with timestamp, region, and tenant ID. Restore monthly into isolated staging, verify migration version, row counts, RLS tests, and signed asset retrieval, then destroy the restore environment. RPO is at most 24 hours and critical-service RTO is under 4 hours.

## AI cost runaway

Disable `ai_enabled`, reject gateway traffic for the organization, retain request accounting, inspect anomalous templates and users, lower quotas, rotate provider credentials if compromised, and re-enable only after Finance and Admin approval.

## Archival

A daily job removes superseded script and asset versions beyond each organization's configured retention only after audit retention and legal-hold checks. Audit logs are never updated or deleted and production exports use WORM storage.

[pgbouncer]
listen_addr = 0.0.0.0
listen_port = 6432
pool_mode = transaction
max_client_conn = 1000
default_pool_size = 40
reserve_pool_size = 10
server_reset_query = DISCARD ALL
server_tls_sslmode = require
client_tls_sslmode = require
ignore_startup_parameters = extra_float_digits

import {defineConfig} from 'cypress';export default defineConfig({e2e:{baseUrl:process.env.WEB_URL??'http://localhost:5173',video:false,retries:1},env:{apiUrl:process.env.API_URL??'http://localhost:4000/api/v1'}});

describe('project creation',()=>{it('creates a timezone-aware project',()=>{cy.request('POST',`${Cypress.env('apiUrl')}/auth/login`,{email:'admin@example.test',password:Cypress.env('seedPassword')}).then(({body})=>{const headers={authorization:`Bearer ${body.access_token}`};cy.request({method:'POST',url:`${Cypress.env('apiUrl')}/orgs/00000000-0000-4000-8000-000000000001/projects`,headers,body:{title:'E2E Feature',timezone:'America/Los_Angeles',start_date:'2026-03-07',end_date:'2026-03-12'}}).its('status').should('eq',201);});});});

name: CI
on: [push, pull_request]
permissions: {contents: read}
jobs:
  validate:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    services:
      postgres:
        image: postgres:16.6-alpine
        env: {POSTGRES_USER: studioforge, POSTGRES_PASSWORD: studioforge, POSTGRES_DB: studioforge}
        ports: [5432:5432]
        options: --health-cmd "pg_isready -U studioforge" --health-interval 5s --health-timeout 5s --health-retries 10
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with: {node-version: 22, cache: npm}
      - run: npm ci
      - run: npm run lint
      - run: npm run typecheck
      - run: npm test
      - run: psql "$DATABASE_URL" -v ON_ERROR_STOP=1 -f migrations/001_initial.sql
        env: {DATABASE_URL: postgresql://studioforge:studioforge@localhost:5432/studioforge}
      - run: npx @redocly/cli@1.27.2 lint openapi/openapi.yaml
      - run: npx @cyclonedx/cyclonedx-npm@2.0.0 --output-file sbom.json
      - run: npm audit --audit-level=high
      - uses: hashicorp/setup-terraform@v3
      - run: terraform fmt -check -recursive infra/terraform

# StudioForge

StudioForge is a multi-tenant film pre-production MVP. It includes a responsive React client, Fastify API, centralized AI gateway, Redis/BullMQ workers, PostgreSQL RLS, signed S3 uploads, optimistic locking, leases, immutable audit storage, OpenAPI contracts, and shared or dedicated-tenant AWS infrastructure.

## Requirements

Node.js 22, npm 10, Docker Compose, PostgreSQL client tools, and Terraform 1.8 or newer are required.

## Installation

1. Copy `.env.example` to `.env` and replace every development credential.
2. Run `npm install`.
3. Run `docker compose up -d`.
4. Create the MinIO bucket named by `S3_BUCKET` using the MinIO console at `http://localhost:9001`.
5. Export `DATABASE_URL`, then run `npm run migrate` and `npm run seed`.
6. Run `npm run dev`. The web client is on port 5173, API on 4000, and AI gateway on 4001.

The committed seed password hash is synthetic and is not accepted as a documented credential. Create a local user through `/api/v1/auth/signup` for interactive development.

## Environment variables

`DATABASE_URL`, `REDIS_URL`, `JWT_SECRET`, `S3_BUCKET`, `AWS_REGION`, and AWS credentials are required. `S3_ENDPOINT` enables MinIO. `ACCESS_TOKEN_TTL` must not exceed 15 minutes. `REFRESH_TOKEN_DAYS` defaults to 30. `AI_GATEWAY_URL` selects the gateway. `AI_PROVIDER_URL` and `AI_PROVIDER_API_KEY` enable an external provider; without them the deterministic local summarizer supports development. `WEB_ORIGIN`, `SENTRY_DSN`, and service-specific `PORT` values are optional. Production secrets must come from AWS Secrets Manager and KMS, never environment files in source control.

## Commands

- `npm run dev`: start local infrastructure and workspace development processes
- `npm run build`: compile every service and frontend
- `npm run lint`: run strict ESLint checks
- `npm run typecheck`: check all TypeScript workspaces
- `npm test`: run unit tests with coverage
- `npm run migrate`: apply the canonical schema and RLS migration
- `npm run seed`: load deterministic staging fixtures

Refresh the dashboard materialized view every five minutes with `REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW CONCURRENTLY dashboard_aggregates`. Run archival and backup jobs daily. Workers are idempotent through the unique organization/idempotency key and move terminal failures to the `dead` state.

## Testing

Unit tests cover deterministic parsing and estimates. Integration RLS checks are in `tests/integration/rls.sql`; execute them using a non-owner `studioforge_app` role because table owners bypass RLS. Cypress acceptance tests are under `tests/e2e`. The synthetic AI regression corpus is under `tests/ai-tests`. CI validates TypeScript, OpenAPI, migrations, Terraform formatting, dependency vulnerabilities, and generates a CycloneDX SBOM.

## Deployment

Build immutable containers for `apps/api`, `apps/ai-gateway`, and `apps/workers`. Deploy the web workspace to Vercel with `VITE_API_URL`. Deploy Node services to ECS, Kubernetes, or another long-lived managed container platform behind TLS. Provision shared infrastructure through `infra/terraform/modules/shared`; invoke `org-provisioner` for customers requiring dedicated regional PostgreSQL and S3 resources. Use RDS Proxy as the managed PgBouncer-equivalent, private subnets, least-privilege IAM, KMS encryption, bucket versioning, daily backups, autoscaling by CPU and queue depth, and CDN access restricted to signed objects.

GitHub pull requests run checks without deployment secrets. Main-branch merges deploy staging and run smoke and restore verification; production promotion is manual after SLO review. Configure OpenTelemetry collectors, Sentry DSNs, Prometheus scraping, Grafana alerts for latency, error rate, queue backlog, AI spend, and database saturation, with PagerDuty or Slack routing.

Operational targets are median non-AI API latency below 300 ms, realtime deltas below two seconds, RPO at most 24 hours, and critical-service RTO below four hours. Incident response, tenant isolation, backup restoration, AI cost containment, and retention procedures are documented in `docs/RUNBOOKS.md`.
